title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that model selection contributes only 10% to AI coding success while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) constitutes the other 90%. The video breaks down the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and contends that context engineering and orchestration skills are the key differentiators for 2026.
writeup: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, framing its central claim that model selection accounts for roughly 10% of AI coding success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, and verification) drives the other 90%. The video maps the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and argues that context engineering and orchestration are the differentiating skills heading into 2026. The takeaway is that investment in the harness, rather than chasing the latest model, determines outcomes.
